scope: Pass B full-surface expansion: every documented resource section (Account, Clients, Campaigns, Lists, Subscribers, Segments, Templates, Transactional) is now accounted for. 13 read streams (the 4 legacy streams plus segments, templates, list_custom_fields, list_webhooks, and 5 subscriber-state fan-out streams: active/unconfirmed/unsubscribed/bounced/deleted subscribers) and 14 write actions covering list/subscriber/segment/campaign lifecycle mutations. Excluded categories: account-level administrative endpoints (admins, billing, primary contact, sending domains) judged out_of_scope for a marketing-data connector; Transactional (a SEPARATE product/API surface - Smart/Classic transactional email sending - requiring its own distinct auth scoping and out of scope for this pass); every single-item detail GET that duplicates an already-covered list stream's shape; campaign-analytics sub-resources (opens/clicks/bounces/unsubscribes/spam/recipients) which are event logs rather than the base campaign object already covered; and the client-creation/deletion/people/billing management surface (agency-administrative, not marketing-data operations).

excluded.reason: account administrator user list, an agency-administrative resource rather than marketing/subscriber data; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
create counterpart of the excluded admins resource; not implemented in this pass
update counterpart of the excluded admins resource; not implemented in this pass
delete counterpart of the excluded admins resource; not implemented in this pass
account billing/credits snapshot, not a syncable marketing-data object
static reference/lookup list of valid country names, not account-specific data
static reference/lookup list of valid timezone names, not account-specific data
returns the API server's current date/time, not a syncable data object
account-level primary-contact designation, an agency-administrative setting rather than marketing/subscriber data; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
update counterpart of the excluded primarycontact resource; not implemented in this pass
initiates an embedded-UI login session token, not a data mutation

creates a new billable client entity (an agency-administrative action provisioning a sub-account), a materially higher-risk/higher-scope action than ordinary marketing-data mutations; out of scope for this pass
returns account-administrative BasicDetails/BillingDetails (company name, credits, billing rates), not marketing/subscriber data; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
returns the same List objects the lists stream already covers, filtered to those containing a given email address; not a distinct resource shape

bulk-suppresses a list of email addresses account-wide; the per-subscriber unsubscribe_subscriber/delete_subscriber writes already cover the practical per-record suppression shapes this dialect can express; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
reverses a bulk suppression; counterpart of the excluded suppress endpoint, not implemented in this pass
agency-administrative client company/timezone/contact settings, not marketing/subscriber data; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
agency billing-plan configuration, not marketing/subscriber data
agency billing-plan configuration, not marketing/subscriber data
transfers billing credits between clients, a financial operation rather than marketing-data mutation

permanently deletes an entire client entity and everything under it (lists, campaigns, subscribers); an agency-administrative action of materially higher blast radius than any covered write; out of scope for this pass
manages agency staff access to a client account, not marketing/subscriber data; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
update counterpart of the excluded people resource; not implemented in this pass
agency staff access list, not marketing/subscriber data; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
delete counterpart of the excluded people resource; not implemented in this pass
client-level primary-contact designation, agency-administrative; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
client-level primary-contact read, agency-administrative; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
returns the static list of subscriber-preference tag names configured for the client, not a syncable record collection
same Campaign object shape as the campaigns stream, filtered to scheduled-but-not-yet-sent status; not a distinct resource
same Campaign object shape as the campaigns stream, filtered to draft status; not a distinct resource
email-authentication (SPF/DKIM) domain configuration, an infrastructure/deliverability setting rather than marketing/subscriber data; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
create counterpart of the excluded sendingdomains resource; not implemented in this pass
delete counterpart of the excluded sendingdomains resource; not implemented in this pass
copies a sending domain configuration between clients; not implemented in this pass
triggers DNS-authentication verification for a sending domain; not implemented in this pass

template-based variant of creating a draft campaign; create_campaign's HtmlUrl-based body already covers draft-campaign creation, and this dialect has no per-action body-shape branching to express a second alternate create body for the same resource
sends a one-off preview copy to a test address, not a mutation of the campaign's own syncable state; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
post-send analytics breakdown by email client, an aggregate report rather than a syncable per-record object; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
returns the ListIDs/SegmentIDs already present on the campaigns stream's own record (the same fields sent when the campaign was created)
post-send recipient event log, not the base campaign object already covered; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
post-send bounce event log, not the base campaign object already covered; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
post-send open event log, not the base campaign object already covered; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
post-send click event log, not the base campaign object already covered; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
post-send unsubscribe event log tied to a specific campaign send, not the base campaign object already covered; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
post-send spam-complaint event log, not the base campaign object already covered; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
post-send aggregate delivery/engagement statistics, not a row-per-record syncable object; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage

single-list detail; the lists stream's own per-item object (ListID/Name) already identifies every list this bundle discovers; the additional settings fields shown here (ConfirmedOptIn/UnsubscribePage/etc.) are the same fields update_list can set, not new discoverable data
aggregate subscriber-count statistics for a list, not a row-per-record syncable object; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
same Segment object shape as the segments stream (fanned out over every client's every list already), filtered to a single list; not a distinct resource
creates a new custom-field DEFINITION (schema change) rather than a data record; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
renames/reconfigures a custom-field definition (schema change), not a data record mutation; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
updates a custom-field's allowed-value option list (schema change), not a data record mutation
deletes a custom-field DEFINITION, permanently discarding that field's data for every subscriber on the list; a schema-destroying action of materially higher blast radius than a per-record delete
registers a new outbound webhook subscription (infrastructure configuration), not a marketing-data mutation; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
fires a one-off test payload at a webhook URL, not a data mutation or read
delete counterpart of the excluded webhook-create endpoint; not implemented in this pass
activates a webhook subscription (infrastructure configuration), not a marketing-data mutation
deactivates a webhook subscription (infrastructure configuration), not a marketing-data mutation
bulk multi-subscriber import in one request; add_subscriber already covers the per-record create shape this dialect's one-request-per-record write model can express; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
per-subscriber campaign-interaction event history, not the base subscriber object already covered by the 5 subscriber-state streams; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
single-subscriber detail (by email query param); identical record shape to the 5 subscriber-state list streams' per-item object, already covered by those streams

adds a single rulegroup to an existing segment; update_segment's full RuleGroups replace already covers reshaping a segment's rules in one write action, and the dialect has no additive-PATCH-vs-full-PUT branching for the same resource
single-segment detail; identical record shape (plus an ActiveSubscribers count) to the segments stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
clears a segment's rules to empty; update_segment (RuleGroups: []) already expresses this exact effect via the general update action
same Subscriber record shape as the active_subscribers stream, filtered to one segment's matching rule set instead of a whole list; not a distinct resource shape
single-template detail (adds the raw HTML content); identical index fields to the templates stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream

requires a full HTML/CSS design payload (HtmlPageURL and a full HtmlPageOrganization spec) which is authoring content, not a plain structured data mutation this dialect's declarative write body is meant to express; out of scope for this pass
same HTML/CSS design-content authoring shape as template creation; out of scope for this pass
deletes a reusable campaign template; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage alongside the excluded template create/update actions
duplicates a template to another client; not implemented in this pass
